steps.title: Identify the angle pair as corresponding angles
steps.content: Lines $m$ and $n$ are parallel, crossed by transversal $t$. The angle of $170°$ is formed at the intersection of $t$ and $m$, while angle $w$ is formed at the intersection of $t$ and $n$. Both angles are positioned identically relative to their intersection points — in the same location with respect to the parallel lines and transversal. These are corresponding angles.

steps.title: Apply the Corresponding Angles Postulate
steps.content: When a transversal intersects two parallel lines, corresponding angles are congruent. Since $170°$ and $w$ are corresponding angles formed by transversal $t$ crossing parallel lines $m$ and $n$: $$w = 170°$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
